prompt:
Our deploy tooling reads and rewrites .env files, and today it does both with regexes that three different people have patched. Last straw: a rewrite turned someone's Windows share path inside double quotes into a value with a real newline in it, and the service came up half-configured. I want a proper reader/writer pair, envfile.ts, no dependencies, with the format rules WRITTEN DOWN. `node --test test_envfile.ts` is acceptance and that file is read-only.

The format spec (this is the whole contract, no clever extras):

parseEnv(text: string): Record<string, string>
- Lines end at \n; a \r immediately before the \n is dropped (CRLF files parse fine). Line numbers are 1-based.
- Blank/whitespace-only lines are skipped, as are lines whose first non-blank character is '#'.
- Otherwise: optional leading blanks, a key matching [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*, optional blanks, '=', optional blanks, then the value. Anything else (bad key, missing '=') is an error.
- Unquoted value: runs to end of line, except a '#' immediately preceded by a space or tab starts a comment; then trailing blanks are trimmed. Backslashes are literal (a two-character \n stays two characters), quotes appearing mid-value are literal, and the value may be empty. If the first non-blank char after '=' is '#', the value is empty and the rest is comment.
- Single-quoted value: everything to the next single quote, completely literal — no escapes exist, '#' and '$' and backslashes are just bytes. 
- Double-quoted value: exactly five escapes are recognized — \n \t \r \" \\ — and decode to newline, tab, CR, quote, backslash; any other backslash sequence is an error. '#' and '$' inside are literal.
- Quotes must close on their own line (an unterminated quote is an error, reported on that line). After a closing quote only blanks are allowed, optionally followed by a '#' comment; anything else is an error.
- '$' is literal EVERYWHERE — this tool never expands variables.
- Duplicate keys: the last assignment wins.
- Errors: throw EnvParseError (exported, extends Error) with a 1-based .line property.

serializeEnv(entries: Record<string, string>): string
- One KEY=value line per entry, insertion order, '\n' after every line (empty object gives the empty string).
- Keys are validated against the same pattern; a bad key throws TypeError.
- Form selection, exactly this ladder: PLAIN if the value is non-empty, has no leading or trailing space/tab, and contains no '#', no quote of either kind, no backslash and no control characters. Else SINGLE-quoted if it contains no single quote and no control characters. Else DOUBLE-quoted, escaping backslash, double quote, newline, tab and CR with their backslash forms. Values containing any other control character (codepoint < 0x20 beyond those three) throw TypeError.
- The point of the ladder: parseEnv(serializeEnv(x)) must be the identity for every representable value — the tests hammer this with paths, apostrophes, hashes, padded and multiline strings.
notes: Authoring-time toolchain: node 26.3 (type stripping). Differentiation: no existing seed covers dotenv-style files; spec is pinned in the prompt, not 'match the dotenv package'.
